title: Train Simulator
notes: Some routes/trains don't work. Some Quick Drive routes tested to work: London-Brighton with Class 377, and HST. West Coast Main Line works with Class 86 and Class 390, though missing some overhead signal gantries (signal itself visible). Hagen Siegen works with ICE 1 and DB101. Isle of Wight works with Class 377, though missing water textures at Ryde Pier Head.

title: Train Simulator
notes: No fonts in main menu.

notes: Quick startup and running fine with sound etc.

title: Train Simulator
notes: Crashes with error: Failed to create dump file 'Z:\TempDump\v62.8b.dmp' (error 3)

title: Train Simulator
notes:
I can confirm that much more of the DLC works, by manually unzipping the .ap files that RailWorks uses for its content. 

Use the following command in your terminal to find the .ap files in your RailWorks folder and unzip them:

find .local/share/Steam/steamapps/common/RailWorks/ -name "*.ap" -execdir unzip '{}' ';'

title: Train Simulator

notes: Some corrupted fonts in HUD, otherwise fine, tested WCML and New York-New Haven  (64-bit version is borked, doesn't even start)
title: Train Simulator 2019

notes: 64 bit works if you use the 64 bit version of OpenAl32, it is located under Install/OpenAL/64bit and copy to the Railworks main folder. 32 bit will no longer work, unless you then use 32 bit OpenAL, to enable that you must use the 32 bit version (located in a similar place as the 64 bit). Also, follow the other users explanation on expanding the .ap files.
title: Train Simulator 2019

notes: I unzipped the DLC-s as advised by others. Game runs mostly fine, but a few Tutorials won't load. Drawing distance seems to be buggy, sometimes hills are missing, some valleys are just black. Very bad aliasing artifacts, but I can't tell for sure whether it's the game itself or Proton, because I have never seen the game on native Windows.
title: Train Simulator 2019

cpu: AMD A8-3850 APU with Radeon HD Graphics
notes:
Steam Play installs all the necessary 3rd party middle-ware (DotNET, PhysX) on start automatically (via Wine-tricksing). 

The ONLY thing the user has to do is use WinZip or 7zip and unpack the so-called ".ap" files (which are regular zips) inside the Railworks/Assets and Railworks/Content/Routes folders. (Something Railworks users are already very familiar with, when they are installing user mods (so called "Reskins").

Game engine runs flawlessly, rendering all the available Shader Effects (incl. dynamic lighting, volumetric smoke, raindrop effects, etc.). Sound engine provides equally all (surround) sound effects. Game logic (Train AI, Train Signalling, Physics, Cab-functionality) LUA scripts all perform as it was a Windows game. FFB works too. Framerate is in the same window as if it were on MS Windows. (Tested with Radeon AND Nvidia).

Tested only the 32-bit version. Since TS2019 the game offers a 64-executable, which comes also with an addition OpenAL 64-bit audio driver.
title: Train Simulator 2019

notes:
I had to unpack the .ap-files in the game folder as stated by others.
Game works as expected on my dedicated gpu (RX550).
The openal - library has to be copied from a folder in the game's directory to the main directory. You can copy a 32-bit or a 64-bit library, it depends on the version of the game, you are planning to use.
No other tweaks should be necessary.
title: Train Simulator 2019

notes:
I have downloaded my Steam-Content, which contains the original ts 2016 plus a lot of routes and locos, furthermore I copied content not buyed from DTG, like virtual Railroads BR101 and BR120, and Software from Armstrong Powerhouse, JustTrains and Aerosoft. 
I drove several routes, like Cologne Koblenz; Koblenz Trier; and some british routes like Waterloo Portsmouth, Isle of Wight, West Highland Line Extension. They run very well without errors, not to say much better than in my Windows 10 PC, but the specs of this Linux computer are much better too. I changed the graphic settings to max in most aspects, and there are no stutters, like I have seen in my windows pc.

It seems to be the 32bit version when I click 64bit, ts does not start.

The only problem at the moment are exe-files which I cannot execute on my linux system. I install them in my windows pc and rsync them - with the bash-extension from windows - to my linux system.
title: Train Simulator 2019
